item.title: James Talarico, Gina Hinojosa attack GOP with parallel populist pitches at Texas Democratic Convention
item.description: The U.S. Senate and gubernatorial nominees accused Republicans, including opponents Ken Paxton and Greg Abbott, of fixating on culture wars to distract from higher costs and alleged graft.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Abbott trolls Talarico with tacos and cattle at Texas Democratic Convention, ignoring opponent Hinojosa
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Mandated Bible stories in reading lists, revamped history for Texas public schools approved
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Democratic land commissioner nominee diagnosed with cancer, announces he’s in remission
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Dolores Huerta tells Texas Democrats to organize and “save democracy” in surprise convention appearance
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Cornyn says closed primaries could backfire on Texas GOP, breaking with Abbott, Paxton
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas Supreme Court rejects lawsuit by survivors of Uvalde school shooting
item.description: Twenty students and five teachers accused DPS and Uvalde County of negligence in the delayed response to the deadly 2022 shooting.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Harris County immigrant legal services program paused as court battle continues
item.description: The Texas Supreme Court has temporarily blocked Harris officials from financially supporting nonprofits that assist immigrants in deportation cases. The state is trying to permanently bar such programs.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Klibanoff

item.title: Hundreds of data centers are coming to Texas. Here’s what you need to know.
item.description: Texas is experiencing an AI-driven data center boom with at least 248 projects planned statewide. Here’s what to know about the industry’s rapid growth and the debate over its impact.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.

item.title: What Texas can learn from Japanese cities that give technology to its senior citizens
item.dc:creator: Terri Langford

item.title: Mexican man dies in ICE custody in Laredo, at least the 20th fatality this year
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas must pay millions to fix errors in Bluebonnet school materials, contract shows
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Tensions with landowners rise as a raft of gas pipelines push through Texas properties

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump administration to revive restrictive immigration policy
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: The U.S. last beat screwworm in 1966. Can current leaders learn from the past’s playbook?
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: As supporters praise Texas’ proposed “Judeo-Christian” curriculum, rabbis say it dismisses Judaism
item.dc:creator: Ellie Ashby

item.title: Census: Texas leads the U.S. in having more kids, but the state’s fertility rate is still falling

item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost one year after deadly flood

item.title: Where is screwworm in Texas? Track cases here.

item.title: Cornyn and Crockett remain top skeptics of their party’s U.S. Senate nominees after primary losses

item.title: USDA defends screwworm response amid congressional fingerpointing
